name: Diomedes
shortDescription: Diomedes was a Greek hero, King of Argos, and one of the most respected warriors in the Trojan War. He was known for his courage, skill in battle, and divine aid from Athena.
description: Diomedes, son of Tydeus and Deipyle, was the King of Argos and a central figure in the Trojan War. His father, Tydeus, was one of the Seven Against Thebes, and Diomedes inherited his father's martial prowess. Diomedes arrived at Troy with eighty ships, leading the Argive forces. During the war, he distinguished himself through his bravery and fighting ability, even wounding the gods Ares and Aphrodite in battle with the aid of Athena. He famously exchanged armor with Glaucus, recognizing their shared ancestral hospitality, demonstrating his adherence to heroic values. After the war, Diomedes faced various trials, including exile from Argos. Accounts vary, but some say he eventually settled in Italy, where he founded several cities and continued to be revered as a hero. His legacy is one of unwavering courage, strategic brilliance, and a commitment to honor, making him a quintessential example of a Greek hero.
abilities: Divine strength and speed (granted by Athena)
